name: Skill Tree
description: A branching upgrade structure that lets players plan long-term ability growth.
design_notes: Skill Tree should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For progression mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use skill tree as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect skill tree to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels their effort is accumulating into lasting power or options.

parameters.name: reward_amount
parameters.description: Progress awarded when the player completes the triggering action.

parameters.name: unlock_threshold
parameters.description: Amount of progress required before the next reward unlocks.

parameters.name: scaling_curve
parameters.description: Curve used to increase cost, reward, or requirement.

edge_cases: Skill Tree is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Skill Tree overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Skill Tree runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Skill Tree state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Skill Tree plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Skill Tree produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Skill Tree strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Skill Tree becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Skill Tree with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Skill Tree as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Skill Tree with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Skill Tree as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.

example_games.title: Path of Exile
example_games.note: Uses a large passive skill tree for build planning.

example_games.title: Diablo II
example_games.note: Uses class skill investment to define play style.
